scope: Public-safe Discord Cockpit UX Study Gate. This template is not proof that Discord is production-ready.

observed_model.source_of_truth: runtime cards, status snapshots, gates, receipts and worker results
observed_model.discord_role: optional projection, request and approval inbox; not canonical state

study_gate.status_snapshot_dependency: #80 factory status snapshot is the canonical state input
study_gate.product_face_dependency: #82 Product Face visual quality gate is required before accepting cockpit UX

limits_reviewed: component count and component message mode
per-route and global API rate limits
message edit and duplicate event idempotency
client_surfaces_reviewed: desktop client scanning and thread navigation
mobile client scanning and notification load
failure_modes_reviewed: free-form chat is mistaken for formal approval
Discord is offline while runtime progresses

operator_comprehension_model.first_5_seconds: The operator sees whether anything needs attention, whether state is fresh, and where the next safe action lives.
operator_comprehension_model.first_30_seconds: The operator can identify project phase, blockers, pending approvals, missing access and the canonical evidence link.
operator_comprehension_model.first_5_minutes: The operator can inspect one project, understand risk and decide whether to approve, wait, grant access or ask for clarification.
operator_comprehension_model.near_zero_friction_rule: Discord is acceptable only for jobs where the operator does not need to hunt across channels, infer freshness or translate casual chat into formal state.

must_measure: operator comprehension in 5 seconds, 30 seconds and 5 minutes
dashboard update latency and edit behavior
notification burden during active multi-agent work

findings.finding: Discord is not accepted as a primary cockpit from setup alone.
findings.impact: A familiar chat UI can hide stale state, duplicate events, noisy updates or ambiguous approvals.
findings.required_fix: Run the Discord Cockpit UX Study and proof pack before accepting any Discord cockpit implementation.

findings.finding: Dense state belongs in a local web cockpit unless Discord proof shows equal or better comprehension.
findings.impact: The operator may need to compare phases, blockers, evidence and approvals faster than Discord channels can support.
findings.required_fix: Use Discord for projection, notification and approvals first; route dense inspection to the local web cockpit.

next_required_actions: Generate a product-specific Discord Control Tower UX Proof Pack before treating Discord as accepted cockpit UX.
Keep #80 status snapshots as canonical state input for Discord projections.
Apply #82 Product Face visual quality review to any visible cockpit surface before acceptance.
Use #84 local web cockpit for dense state, comparison, inspection and long-running control.
